analysis.mistake: Focus-visible styling didn't match hover styling (missing text-accent), outline color override failed due to unlayered global rule, and dropdown min-width didn't account for padding.
analysis.expected: Focus-visible states should have identical visual treatment as hover states for keyboard accessibility. CSS utilities should override global styles. Dropdown sizing should include padding in width calculations.
analysis.rootCause: Developer didn't check if a global *:focus-visible rule existed or whether it was properly layered. An unlayered rule in body.css defeats @layer utilities rules regardless of specificity. Additionally, when adding hover:text-accent, the developer didn't apply the same styling to focus-visible for keyboard users.

suggestions.content: Focus-visible states must match hover state styling. When adding hover:class to an element, also add focus-visible:class with identical styling. This ensures keyboard users see the same visual feedback as mouse users.
suggestions.rationale: Prevents inconsistent keyboard/mouse feedback and ensures accessibility parity between input methods.

suggestions.content: When adding focus-visible or outline overrides to components, check if a global *:focus-visible rule exists in body.css or other base stylesheets. If found, ensure it is wrapped in @layer base (or the same @layer as your override). Unlayered global rules always beat @layer rules regardless of specificity. Use taktician_screenshot with keyboard actions to verify focus styling works on rendered pages.
suggestions.rationale: CSS cascade layers have strict precedence: unlayered rules > @layer base > @layer components > @layer utilities. An unlayered global rule will silently override Tailwind utility classes unless the rule itself is moved into a @layer.

suggestions.content: When implementing focus-visible or focus-ring styles, search the entire theme for global *:focus-visible, *:focus, or outline rules (especially in body.css, global.css, or reset stylesheets). If a global rule exists outside a @layer, move it into @layer base. If you can't modify the global rule, use !important or re-declare your style with @layer base to ensure it takes precedence. Test with taktician_screenshot keyboard actions on the rendered page — never rely on code review alone.
suggestions.rationale: Prevents silent failures where CSS utilities appear correct in code but fail to override unlayered global rules at runtime.

suggestions.content: When testing focus-visible states, always compare them visually to hover states using taktician_screenshot with keyboard Tab actions. Verify the focus outline color, text color, and background color match the hover state. Don't skip this check even if code review shows matching classes — CSS precedence issues can cause focus styles to fail at runtime while hover styles work.
suggestions.rationale: Ensures focus-visible styling actually renders as intended, catching CSS layer precedence issues that code review can't detect.
